study_group_representative.quote: We estimated a time-varying reproduction number, Rt, to assess the effectiveness of control measures employed in 10 selected counties.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: We employed a method proposed by Cori et al.1 to estimate Rt in 10 selected countries, namely Belgium, China, France, Germany, Iran, South Korea, Spain, Thailand, USA and UK

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: Figure 1A—J illustrates the time series of the estimated Rt on weekly windows with 95% confidence interval (CI) in 10 selected countries

follow_up_length_reported.quote: starting from the date at which the number of cumulative confirmed cases in each country exceeds 100... as of 7 April 2020

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: This Rt estimation method only requires the number of daily new cases and the distribution of the serial interval, which is the time between the onset of symptoms of a primary case and the onset of symptoms of secondary cases, as an input

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: The time-varying reproduction number, Rt, is the average number of secondary cases of disease caused by an infected individual over his or her infectious period.

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: Finally, country-to-country comparisons of the effectiveness of control measures are problematic due to many confounding factors, such as different population sizes and densities, superspreading events, social mixing patterns and nuances in social distancing measures.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: We also calculated the reduction in Rt (ΔRt): a higher ΔRt is indicative of more successful control measures.
